summary: Russia conducted a massive combined missile and drone strike on June 2, 2026 targeting Kyiv, Dnipro, Kharkiv, and Zaporizhzhia, killing at least 7 in the capital and 8 in Dnipro including a child. Ukrainian forces launched retaliatory drone attacks on Russian territory including St. Petersburg (Kronstadt, Kirovsky, Krasnoselsky districts), Voronezh, Tambov (Michurinsk), and Belgorod regions, with a drone strike on a passenger bus in DNR killing 7 civilians. Russia's Northern Fleet nuclear submarine Arkhangelsk conducted a successful Oniks cruise missile test firing in the Barents Sea at a range of 200 km.
key_points: Massive Russian combined missile-drone strike hit Kyiv, Dnipro, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia; 7 killed in Kyiv, 8 in Dnipro including a child (Source: @UkraineNow/81636, @UkraineNow/81609)

Ukrainian drones struck St. Petersburg areas (Kronstadt, Kirovsky, Krasnoselsky districts), Voronezh region, and Tambov region (Michurinsk) causing damage and casualties (Source: @tass_agency/378746, @tass_agency/378738, @tass_agency/378718)
Ukrainian drone attack on a Moscow-Simferopol passenger bus in DNR killed 7 civilians and wounded 11, per Pushilin (Source: @tass_agency/378736)
Russia's nuclear submarine Arkhangelsk successfully test-fired an Oniks cruise missile at 200 km range in the Barents Sea (Source: @tass_agency/378720)
Ukrainian Air Forces shot down only 11 of 33 Iskander ballistic missiles and 0 of 8 Tsirkons; enemy changed tactics with staggered follow-up strikes (Source: @UkraineNow/81612)
Multiple UAV threats tracked across Chernihiv, Rivne, Zhytomyr, Dnipropetrovsk, Mykolaiv, Kharkiv, and Sumy regions throughout the night (Source: @kpszsu/63714, @kpszsu/63746, @eRadarrua/95135)
High threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk region was declared; Pulkovo airport operations temporarily restricted (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67466, @tass_agency/378723)

sections.executive_summary: The most operationally significant development is the massive Russian combined missile and drone strike on June 2, 2026 targeting Kyiv, Dnipro, Kharkiv, and Zaporizhzhia, which killed at least 15 civilians including a child and hit a Ukroboronprom building and energy infrastructure. Ukraine responded with widespread drone attacks deep into Russian territory including St. Petersburg, Voronezh, Tambov, and border regions, while also striking a passenger bus in DNR. Russia simultaneously demonstrated strategic deterrence through a successful Oniks cruise missile test from a nuclear submarine in the Barents Sea.

points: Ukrainian Air Forces intercepted only 11 of 33 Iskander ballistic missiles and 0 of 8 Tsirkons during the June 2 massive attack, with the enemy employing staggered follow-up strikes to exploit air defense reloading windows (Source: @UkraineNow/81612)

A Ukroboronprom building in Kyiv caught fire after a Russian missile strike, and an energy facility was hit causing power outages across the capital (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30603, @ukr_leaks_eng/30607)
In Dnipro, 8 people were killed including a child born in 2023, with bodies recovered from rubble after a Russian strike on the city (Source: @UkraineNow/81609)
In Kyiv, 90 people were injured from the June 2 mass attack, with 51 hospitalized including 2 children, and 7 people killed according to Mayor Klitschko (Source: @UkraineNow/81636)
Ukrainian defense industry enterprises in the Khmelnytskyi region caught fire following Russian attacks (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30611)
President Zelensky warned that intelligence indicates a massive strike could occur again tonight, urging citizens to pay attention to air raid alerts (Source: @UkraineNow/81634)

Spokesperson Trehubov stated Russian forces lack the capacity for simultaneous large-scale offensives across multiple front sectors and are shifting to localized operations (Source: @UkraineNow/81621)
The nuclear submarine Arkhangelsk of Russia's Northern Fleet successfully test-fired an Oniks cruise missile at a sea target at 200 km range in the Barents Sea (Source: @tass_agency/378720)
Seven civilian drones were shot down over Voronezh region overnight, with two houses damaged according to the regional governor (Source: @tass_agency/378738)
Thirty drones were intercepted over Leningrad region according to the regional governor; Pulkovo airport operations were temporarily restricted (Source: @tass_agency/378724, @tass_agency/378723)
Ukrainian drones struck infrastructure in Kronstadt, Kirovsky, and Krasnoselsky districts of St. Petersburg, with several people injured (Source: @tass_agency/378746)
Ukrainian drones attacked Michurinsk in Tambov region, damaging an apartment building, a library, and a school of arts (Source: @tass_agency/378718)

A Ukrainian drone struck a passenger bus on the Moscow-Simferopol route in DNR, killing 7 civilians and wounding 11 according to Pushilin; a terrorism criminal case was opened (Source: @tass_agency/378736, @tass_agency/378753)
In Krasnodar region, a fire broke out at the Ilskoy refinery due to a UAV attack, and UAV debris fell on a multi-story building in Slavyansk-on-Kuban (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)
On the Kharkiv direction, Russian assault units of the North Group of Forces advanced toward the AFU logistics center of Kachiy Lopan, with 3 AFU soldiers surrendering (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)
On the Volchansk section, Russian assault units of the North Group advanced up to 400 meters and are engaged in firefights in the village of Okhrikovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)
From Kupyansk, Russian zone of control in the built-up area has expanded despite increased Ukrainian UAV use (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)
In Konstantinovka, the enemy admits further deterioration of the situation for the AFU in the built-up area despite increased Ukrainian aircraft use (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)

In the Dobropolsky direction, Russian forces continue operations in settlements adjacent to Grishino: Mirnye, Vasilyevka, and Shevchenko, slowly advancing and consolidating (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)
In eastern Zaporizhzhia region, Russian units are engaged in battles near the village of Komsomolskoe (Gulaypolskoe), with the objective of reaching Orekhov (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)
On the Sumy direction in the Shostka district, units of the North Group of Forces are engaged in firefights in Bachivsk and surrounding areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)
In the Sumy region, Russian forces advanced up to 11 sections of up to 500 meters and are fighting in Ivolzhansky and Pisarevsky (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)

In the Kursk region, a civilian car was hit by a Ukrainian drone in Shchekino village killing one civilian; three people were injured by a UAV attack near a store in Pushkarnoe village (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)

In the Bryansk region, Ukrainian FPV drones attacked a pig farm in Klimovsky district injuring a worker, and injured two women in Andreykovichi village in Pogarsky district (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)

In Belgorod region, a man was injured by shrapnel from an FPV drone attack on a car on the Besnovka-Solozhki road; multiple settlements including Shebekino and Dobrovoe are under attack (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)

Multiple UAV groups tracked across Chernihiv region heading toward Slavutych, Meny, and Koriukivka, with additional threats over Zhytomyr region heading toward Rivne (Source: @kpszsu/63746, @kpszsu/63714, @eRadarrua/95142)

In Dnipropetrovsk region, Ukrainian forces are conducting offensive actions toward Alexandrovsk, consolidating and expanding a bridgehead north of the Volchya River in the forest area (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)
A group of UAVs was tracked in Dnipropetrovsk region heading toward Solone, and another group tracked over Nikopol district (Source: @kpszsu/63737, @eRadarrua/95091)
In Mykolaiv region, a group of UAVs was tracked heading toward Snihurivka (Source: @kpszsu/63725)
Crimea and Sevastopol repelled a serious night Ukrainian drone attack (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)
In Rostov region, more than ten UAVs were destroyed in Taganrog and four districts: Chertkovsky, Millerovsky, Tarasovsky, and Bobkovsky (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)
Russia launched 33 Iskander ballistic missiles and 8 Tsirkon missiles in the June 2 mass strike; Ukraine intercepted only 11 Iskanders and 0 Tsirkons (Source: @UkraineNow/81612)

Russia changed attack tactics by launching staggered follow-up strikes in the morning after the main nighttime barrage, targeting Ukrainian air defense during reloading and repositioning windows (Source: @UkraineNow/81612)
A high threat of Iskander-M ballistic missile launches from Bryansk region was declared during the monitoring period (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67466)
Multiple shahed-type drones were tracked heading toward Kyiv from the north, with 1 and then 3 additional shaheds reported in separate updates (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67458, @mon1tor_ua/67464)
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aviation bombs on Sumy region and Donetsk region (Source: @kpszsu/63690)

Ukrainian UAVs conducted widespread attacks across Russian territory with groups tracked over Chernihiv, Zhytomyr, Rivne, Dnipropetrovsk, Mykolaiv, Kharkiv, and Sumy regions (Source: @kpszsu/63714, @eRadarrua/95142, @kpszsu/63740)

key_developments: Russia conducted a massive combined missile and drone strike on June 2 targeting Kyiv, Dnipro, Kharkiv, and Zaporizhzhia, killing at least 7 in Kyiv and 8 in Dnipro including a child, with 90 injured in the capital. A Ukroboronprom building and energy infrastructure were hit. (Source: @UkraineNow/81636, @UkraineNow/81609, @ukr_leaks_eng/30607)

Ukrainian Air Forces intercepted only 11 of 33 Iskander ballistic missiles and 0 of 8 Tsirkons; Russia employed new staggered follow-up strike tactics to exploit air defense reloading windows. (Source: @UkraineNow/81612)

Ukrainian drones struck deep into Russian territory including St. Petersburg (Kronstadt, Kirovsky, Krasnoselsky districts), Voronezh, Tambov (Michurinsk), Krasnodar (Ilskoy refinery), and Belgorod regions, causing damage and casualties. (Source: @tass_agency/378746, @tass_agency/378738, @tass_agency/378718, @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)

A Ukrainian drone attack on a Moscow-Simferopol passenger bus in DNR killed 7 civilians and wounded 11, prompting Russia to open a terrorism criminal case. (Source: @tass_agency/378736, @tass_agency/378753)

Russia's Northern Fleet nuclear submarine Arkhangelsk successfully test-fired an Oniks cruise missile at a sea target at 200 km range in the Barents Sea, demonstrating strategic deterrence capabilities. (Source: @tass_agency/378720)

On the eastern front, Russian forces advanced up to 400 meters on the Volchansk section, expanded control near Kupyansk, and are pushing toward Kachiy Lopan and Orekhov, while Ukrainian forces consolidated a bridgehead north of the Volchya River near Alexandrovsk. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30605)

Multiple Ukrainian UAV groups were tracked across Chernihiv, Zhytomyr, Rivne, Dnipropetrovsk, Mykolaiv, Kharkiv, and Sumy regions, with a high threat of Iskander-M launches from Bryansk region declared and Pulkovo airport operations restricted. (Source: @kpszsu/63746, @mon1tor_ua/67466, @tass_agency/378723)
sections.threat_assessment: The conflict is at a high escalation point with both sides conducting strategic strikes deep into each other's territory. Russia's use of staggered missile strike tactics targeting Ukrainian air defense reloading cycles represents a significant tactical evolution that is degrading Ukraine's interception rates. Ukraine's ability to conduct widespread drone attacks across multiple Russian regions simultaneously demonstrates growing long-range strike capacity, though the strike on the civilian bus in DNR raises concerns about targeting accuracy. The Russian Oniks submarine test signals continued strategic deterrence posturing.
sections.regional_response: Poland's deputy parliamentary speaker called for blocking Ukraine's EU accession over the UPA hero commemoration scandal and urged Poland to stop funding Starlink services for Ukrainian military. Hungary's Prime Minister Magyar offered to meet Zelensky conditional on resolving the status of the Hungarian language in Ukraine. Armenia is redirecting agricultural exports previously blocked by Russia to the EU market, with the first shipments of roses and vegetables already sent.
sections.intelligence_notes: Ukrainian military sources acknowledge poor air defense performance during the June 2 attack, attributing the missile defense gap to insufficient support from Western partners. Russian forces are reportedly unable to conduct simultaneous large-scale offensives across multiple front sectors due to manpower shortages, shifting to localized operations. Internal Ukrainian issues include TCC mobilization abuses, with reports of a 61-year-old Afghan war veteran being beaten and a German citizen stripped of Ukrainian citizenship being sent to a training center.
